stories.headline: Manus 'My Computer' brings Meta's AI agent onto personal laptops
stories.summary: Meta-owned Manus launched its desktop app on March 18, moving from cloud-only to local: 'My Computer' lets Manus agents read, edit, analyze files, and control applications directly on Mac and Windows, with explicit user approval before each action. Paid plans start at $20/month; unlike OpenClaw (which it now directly competes with), Manus is not open source.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to 18th-century locksmithing trade
stories.lateral_explanation: Moving AI agents from cloud to local is structurally identical to the shift from master locksmiths to household keys: the capability migrates from a centralized service provider onto personal property, changing who controls access at the point of use. The privacy model flips accordingly — instead of

stories.headline: Hunter Alpha: mystery trillion-parameter model revealed as Xiaomi, not DeepSeek
stories.summary: The anonymous 1-trillion-parameter model that appeared on OpenRouter on March 11 — processing 160 billion tokens in its first week — was identified by Japan Times on March 19 as originating from Xiaomi, not DeepSeek as widely speculated. The model offered free access, reasoning capabilities, and a 1M-token context window; both Xiaomi and OpenRouter had refused to comment since its appearance.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to naval acoustic intelligence
stories.lateral_explanation: Releasing a major model anonymously and watching where the speculation lands is functionally identical to the US Navy practice of deliberately misattributing signals intelligence — you learn more from observing how adversaries respond to misinformation than from the release itself. Xiaomi now knows

stories.headline: Tencent announces Hunyuan 3.0 for April and builds dedicated AI agent for WeChat
stories.summary: Tencent plans to ship Hunyuan 3.0 in April 2026 and has confirmed development of an advanced AI agent integrated directly into WeChat — the super-app used by 1.3 billion people for messaging, payments, and daily commerce. If deployed at full scale, WeChat's AI agent would become the highest-reach AI agent deployment in history by active user base.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Ottoman bazaar merchant guilds
stories.lateral_explanation: WeChat's architecture already bundles payments, messaging, transport booking, and small business commerce into a single interface — the exact structure of Ottoman bazaar guilds, which controlled not just trade but the infrastructure through which trade occurred. Embedding an AI agent into WeChat doe

stories.headline: Google expands Gemini 3.1 Pro to Vertex AI enterprise and Gemini CLI
stories.summary: Google made Gemini 3.1 Pro available in Vertex AI preview and Gemini Enterprise, bringing its benchmark-leading model — 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2, 94.3% on GPQA Diamond, $2/M input tokens — into enterprise cloud infrastructure with a 1M-token context window. The Gemini CLI extension brings the same model to developer terminals alongside Vertex AI Agent Designer, now in preview.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Roman road maintenance contracts
stories.lateral_explanation: Gemini 3.1 Pro's expansion into Vertex AI infrastructure follows the same logic as Roman road maintenance contracts: the real competitive advantage wasn't building the fastest chariot but owning the roads every chariot had to use. Google Cloud providing Gemini across CLI, enterprise, and Vertex mean

stories.headline: Microsoft BitNet.cpp: official 1-bit LLM inference framework released on GitHub
stories.summary: Microsoft released BitNet.cpp under the MIT license — a framework for running 1-bit quantized LLMs locally, reducing memory requirements by up to 10x versus full-precision models and enabling capable models on laptops without dedicated GPUs. The repo trended on GitHub the week of March 15, positioning Microsoft as the first major lab to formally release an open 1-bit inference stack.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Morse code compression theory
stories.lateral_explanation: 1-bit quantization is the extreme logical conclusion of Shannon's information theory: reduce each weight to a binary signal, discard everything the model can recover from context. Morse code achieved surprising expressive range with just dots and dashes — not because binary is inherently limited but

stories.headline: Hindsight: vectorize-io ships learning agent memory framework on GitHub
stories.summary: Vectorize-io's Hindsight project — an open framework giving AI agents persistent, searchable episodic memory that learns from past interactions — trended on GitHub the week of March 15. Unlike static context windows, Hindsight allows agents to retrieve and apply lessons from earlier sessions to new tasks, addressing one of the key missing capabilities in current production deployments.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to episodic memory psychology
stories.lateral_explanation: Cognitive science distinguishes semantic memory (general knowledge) from episodic memory (what happened to me, when). Current LLMs have strong semantic memory but no episodic layer — they cannot remember what they did last Tuesday for a specific user. Hindsight is effectively building the episodic m

stories.headline: Grok 4.20: xAI introduces four-agent parallel processing architecture
stories.summary: xAI's Grok 4.20 restructured inference into four parallel agents: Grok coordinates the overall response, Harper handles real-time fact-checking via X data, Benjamin manages logic and code, and Lucas covers creative reasoning — all running simultaneously and merging outputs rather than processing sequentially. Context expanded to 128K tokens with training data current through January 2026.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to jazz quartet improvisation
stories.lateral_explanation: Grok 4.20's four-agent architecture mirrors the structural logic of a jazz quartet: each instrument plays in its own register and rhythm, but the harmonic result only emerges from their simultaneous interaction, not from any player's solo. Unlike sequential reasoning chains — where each step waits f

stories.headline: GitHub 2026: AI coding tools shift open-source contribution from Silicon Valley to global
stories.summary: InfoQ's March 2026 analysis of GitHub's annual data found that AI coding tools have dramatically accelerated open-source contributions from non-English-speaking developers, with over 40% of 2026 AI repository contributions originating from China, India, South Korea, and Brazil. AI tools that auto-generate documentation, respond to English code reviews, and translate issue comments are credited with lowering the language barrier that historically concentrated open source in English-speaking regions.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Gutenberg's printing press
stories.lateral_explanation: Gutenberg's press is famous for democratizing text, but its deeper effect was democratizing participation in knowledge production — previously only Latin-literate monks could contribute. AI coding tools are achieving an analogous shift: for the first time, developers who cannot write fluent English

stories.headline: Vertex AI Agent Engine Memory Bank goes Generally Available
stories.summary: Google's Vertex AI Agent Engine Sessions and Memory Bank reached GA this week, enabling enterprise agents built on Google Cloud to retain context, decisions, and learned preferences across conversations and sessions. The feature addresses one of production agents' most persistent limitations: the inability to remember prior interactions with the same user or task.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to patient medical record systems
stories.lateral_explanation: Persistent agent memory is structurally what the hospital medical record system did for patient care in the 1970s: before standardized records, every doctor visit started from scratch. The same patient described their symptoms again and again to each new physician. Vertex AI's Memory Bank solves the

stories.headline: Apple reimagines Siri with Google's 1.2-trillion-parameter Gemini on Private Cloud Compute
stories.summary: Apple confirmed a fundamental Siri overhaul for 2026: Siri will run Google's Gemini (1.2T parameters) via Private Cloud Compute, routing inference through Apple's infrastructure to preserve privacy guarantees while gaining on-screen awareness, cross-app integration, and context-aware reasoning. The arrangement marks the first time Apple has bet its flagship consumer AI product on a competitor's foundation model.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Swiss watchmakers outsourcing movements
stories.lateral_explanation: In the 1970s, Swiss luxury watchmakers faced a choice: build their own quartz movements at enormous cost or license movements from Japanese manufacturers and focus resources on case design and brand experience. Most chose licensing and survived; those who refused to outsource the movement — the invi

stories.headline: IBM completes $11B Confluent acquisition: real-time data becomes the fuel for enterprise AI agents
stories.summary: IBM closed its acquisition of Confluent on March 17 — a data streaming platform used by 6,500 enterprises including 40% of the Fortune 500 — making real-time event streams the foundational data layer for IBM's watsonx AI agents. The deal addresses the key barrier enterprise agents hit in production: decisions based on stale batch data rather than live operational feeds.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to live ammunition logistics in warfare
stories.lateral_explanation: Military strategists distinguish between intelligence (what was true at briefing time) and live battlefield awareness (what is true right now). Decisions made on stale intelligence consistently produce worse outcomes than those made on real-time data, regardless of how sophisticated the decision mod

stories.headline: IBM's AskHR agent automates 94% of simple HR tasks, delivering $3.5B productivity in two years
stories.summary: IBM's internal AI deployment report documents $3.5 billion in productivity gains over two years by applying AI agents to 70+ business functions, with AskHR automating 94% of simple HR transactions — vacation requests, pay statements, benefits queries — without human involvement. IBM positions itself as 'Client Zero': the enterprise case study proving what its own products can deliver before selling them to clients.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to self-service bank tellers (ATMs)
stories.lateral_explanation: When ATMs launched in the 1970s, banks marketed them as customer convenience. The less publicized truth was that ATMs automated 94% of simple teller transactions, enabling banks to redeploy human tellers to complex advisory work — the same framing IBM uses for AskHR. The structural parallel is preci

stories.headline: Salesforce Agentforce reaches 18,500 enterprise customers and 3 billion automated workflows monthly
stories.summary: Salesforce's Q3 FY26 report reveals Agentforce now serves 18,500 enterprise customers running more than 3 billion automated workflows per month, with agentic product ARR past $540 million — a 114% year-over-year gain. Over 9,500 paid Agentforce deals and 3.2 trillion tokens processed make it the fastest-growing product in Salesforce's history.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Ford assembly line adoption curves
stories.lateral_explanation: When Ford introduced the moving assembly line in 1913, adoption by the broader auto industry followed a classic S-curve: slow initially as manufacturers adapted factory layouts and worker training, then explosively fast once the efficiency gains were undeniable and competitive pressure mounted. Sale

stories.headline: Adecco Group signs unlimited Agentforce 360 license for global AI deployment through 2027
stories.summary: The Adecco Group — the world's largest staffing firm — signed an unlimited global access agreement with Salesforce for Agentforce 360 through 2027, with its three global business units accelerating AI agent deployment across multiple markets. A staffing company using AI agents to match, screen, and manage human workers is among the clearest illustrations of AI agents entering the labor market as a structural layer.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to temp agency economics in the 1970s
stories.lateral_explanation: The temp agency model, pioneered in the 1970s, created a layer of abstraction between employers and workers: the agency handled matching, screening, and compliance while employers got flexible labor without administrative burden. Adecco now deploys AI agents to do exactly what human agency staff onc

stories.headline: Salesforce launches Agentforce Contact Center: AI agents unify voice, digital, and CRM for enterprise service
stories.summary: Salesforce's new Agentforce Contact Center (March 2026) integrates AI agents with voice calls, digital channels, and live CRM data into a single enterprise customer service system. Early deployments show agents handling complex multi-channel service interactions end-to-end — not just routing tickets but resolving them — with human escalation only for edge cases.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to 19th-century telegraph switching operators
stories.lateral_explanation: Telegraph switching operators in the 1880s performed an exact analog of what a contact center agent does: they received a message on one channel, interpreted intent, and routed it to the correct destination, occasionally escalating to a human supervisor for ambiguous cases. The difference is latency

stories.headline: Fusemachines AI Interview Agent at Talent Connects: 300+ businesses get AI-scored hiring
stories.summary: Talent Connects (a recruiting platform serving 300+ businesses) integrated Fusemachines' Interview Agent into its hiring workflows on March 18, giving employers AI-driven interview guidance, real-time candidate scoring, and structured evaluation reports. The deployment makes AI-conducted and AI-scored interviews standard hiring infrastructure for hundreds of small and mid-size employers simultaneously.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to civil service examination standardization
stories.lateral_explanation: The Chinese imperial examination system — standardized for over 1,300 years — solved the same problem AI interview agents address: how to evaluate candidates at scale without the quality varying based on who administers the test. The difference is that imperial exams reduced human bias through stand

stories.headline: Tencent's WeChat AI agent will give 1.3 billion users an autonomous daily workflow assistant
stories.summary: Alongside Hunyuan 3.0, Tencent confirmed development of a dedicated AI agent inside WeChat that will handle scheduling, task execution, merchant interactions, and service bookings across its 1.3 billion user base. The architecture leverages WeChat's mini-program ecosystem — over 3 million apps — as the agent's action layer, giving it a larger pre-integrated tool library than any Western equivalent.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Japanese kaizen continuous improvement
stories.lateral_explanation: Toyota's kaizen philosophy holds that the worker closest to the process has the best information for improving it — but the knowledge must be systematically captured and institutionalized, not lost when the worker leaves. A WeChat AI agent that observes 1.3 billion daily workflows and iterates on ta

stories.headline: HBR: companies must hire 'Agent Managers' — humans who direct, audit, and escalate AI worker fleets
stories.summary: Harvard Business Review (February 2026) documents the emergence of 'Agent Manager' as a functional role: the human responsible for defining agent goals, reviewing outputs, managing escalations, and measuring ROI across a fleet of AI workers. The article argues this role is more strategic than technical — closer to operations management than engineering — and predicts it will become one of the most in-demand enterprise positions by 2027.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to air traffic control systems design
stories.lateral_explanation: Air traffic control wasn't invented because planes couldn't fly themselves — it was invented because the sky filled with planes fast enough that no individual pilot had situational awareness of the whole system. Agent Managers are being invented for the same reason: not because AI agents can't compl

stories.headline: Gartner: 57% of companies have AI agents in production; 15% of work decisions autonomous by 2028
stories.summary: Gartner's 2026 enterprise AI report finds 57% of companies already running AI agents in production, up from near-zero in 2024, with a projection that 15% of day-to-day work decisions will be made autonomously by 2028. The same report warns that 40% of agentic projects will fail by 2027 because legacy systems lack real-time APIs, modular architectures, and secure identity management for non-human agents.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to electricity grid adoption in factories
stories.lateral_explanation: When electrical grids became available in the 1890s, factories that adopted electricity first didn't immediately see productivity gains — because they simply swapped steam engines for electric motors while keeping the same factory layout. The productivity boom came 20-30 years later when factories w

stories.headline: CIO deep-dive: AI agents deliver fast ROI only in constrained domains — governance lags dangerously
stories.summary: CIO.com's March 2026 analysis finds that only 'constrained, well-governed domains' — IT incident response, finance reconciliation, employee onboarding, and audit — are delivering the fast ROI promised by AI workforce proponents, while cross-functional autonomous agents remain unreliable at scale. The report documents that only 1 in 5 companies has a mature governance model for autonomous agents despite 57% having them in production.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to nuclear power plant containment zones
stories.lateral_explanation: Nuclear power plants generate enormous value but only when operated within precisely defined containment parameters — attempt to run them outside those parameters and the risks become unmanageable. AI agents in enterprise follow the same logic: they generate outsized ROI in well-bounded domains (def

stories.headline: Fernando Aramburu — «Maite»: ETA, 1997, y los silencios que destruyen una familia
stories.summary: Aramburu regresa con una novela ambientada en San Sebastián en julio de 1997 — el mes del asesinato de Miguel Ángel Blanco — que sigue a dos hermanas, Maite y Elene, durante cuatro días de reencuentro marcados por verdades no dichas y la sombra del terrorismo. A diferencia de Patria, que abordaba la tragedia colectiva, Maite excava en el daño privado que la violencia política deja en las relaciones familiares.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to trauma archaeology in forensic science
stories.lateral_explanation: Forensic archaeologists excavating mass graves have found that physical evidence often tells a different story than official accounts — bones record what language suppresses. Aramburu's narrative method in Maite is structurally identical: the novel excavates what the family never says aloud, treatin

stories.headline: Han Kang — «Tinta y Sangre»: el thriller de la Nobel llega al español por primera vez
stories.summary: Se publica por primera vez en español Tinta y Sangre (2010), la novela noir de Han Kang en la que una investigadora obsesionada sigue la muerte de su amiga pintora en un recorrido que mezcla el duelo, el crimen y la creación artística. Con el Premio Nobel de 2024, esta primera traducción española llega con la urgencia de recuperar toda su obra anterior para los lectores hispanohablantes.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to posthumous musical scores discovery
stories.lateral_explanation: When composers die, musicologists scramble to recover unpublished scores — not because the music is necessarily better than the known catalogue, but because each recovered piece reshapes understanding of the whole body of work. Han Kang's earlier novels arriving in Spanish for the first time after t

stories.headline: Sergio del Molino — «La hija»: Rosario Weiss, la discípula de Goya borrada de la historia del arte
stories.summary: Frente al autorretrato de Rosario Weiss en el Museo del Prado, Del Molino reconstruye la vida de la pintora — ahijada y discípula de Goya — borrada de la historia del arte para no empañar el mito del maestro. La hija es una novela de reparación histórica y una reflexión sobre cómo los grandes nombres consumen a quienes los rodean.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to satellite galaxy occlusion in astronomy
stories.lateral_explanation: In astronomy, satellite galaxies are frequently undetectable when observed near a larger galaxy because the dominant light source overwhelms the sensors — the satellite exists, but measuring it requires blocking the primary. Historians of art have done the same to Rosario Weiss: she was always there

stories.headline: Sara Barquinero — «La chica más lista que conozco»: filosofía, Madrid y la violencia del aula universitaria
stories.summary: Barquinero estructura su segunda novela como un tratado filosófico: Alicia llega a Madrid desde Valladolid a estudiar filosofía y descubre que el aula universitaria replica las mismas jerarquías de poder que pretende cuestionar. El libro denuncia el abuso de autoridad en la academia y la falta de solidaridad entre mujeres en entornos masculinizados, con una voz narrativa fría y precisa.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Milgram obedience experiments
stories.lateral_explanation: Stanley Milgram's 1961 experiments demonstrated that institutional authority — the white coat, the university setting, the official title — reliably induced otherwise ethical people to harm others. Barquinero's novel is set in the same apparatus: the university's authority structure doesn't just fai

stories.headline: Benjamín Labatut — «La Antártica empieza aquí»: el primer libro del autor de MANIAC, reeditado por Anagrama
stories.summary: Anagrama reedita el debut de Labatut — seis relatos ganadores del Premio Caza de Letras 2009 — en los que la realidad se deshace y decisiones mínimas lo alteran todo. El relato que da título al libro sigue a un periodista en busca de un poeta con pasado nazi candidato al Premio Nacional, hasta una expedición a la Antártida.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to fossil record gap theory
stories.lateral_explanation: Paleontologists call it the 'Lagerstätten problem': fossilization is so rare that the fossil record is almost entirely gap, and our understanding of evolutionary history is built on a few exceptional preservation events. Labatut's debut functions as literary Lagerstätten — a rare preservation of the

stories.headline: Lorenzo Silva — «Con nadie»: el general Campins y la ética como condena en la España de 1936
stories.summary: Lorenzo Silva rescata al general Miguel Campins — huérfano desde la infancia, veterano de las guerras de Marruecos — que eligió sus principios en el verano de 1936 y pagó con su vida. Con nadie es una novela de historia y de carácter, sobre la soledad de quien no traiciona sus convicciones cuando todos a su alrededor lo hacen.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to game theory prisoner's dilemma
stories.lateral_explanation: In iterated prisoner's dilemma experiments, cooperation only survives if enough players around you also cooperate — a single cooperative agent surrounded by defectors will always lose. Campins's tragedy is precisely this: in the summer of 1936, the cooperative strategy (loyalty to constitutional ord

books.title: The AI Agent Revolution: How Autonomous Systems Are Reshaping Enterprise
books.author: Rakesh Gohel
books.why: Directly addresses the agentic AI transformation David has been tracking all week — from IBM's $3.5B productivity gains to Salesforce's 18,500 enterprise customers. Gohel combines the theoretical framework with enterprise case studies across banking and insurance, industries David's digest heavily covers.

books.title: Money Machine: How AI Payments Are Reshaping Global Finance
books.author: Melanie Swan
books.why: Capitalizes on David's strong interest in AI/payments marked in feedback by examining how foundation models trained on transaction data unlock new financial services. Directly applicable to understanding the payments-AI nexus David has been observing this week.
